av2mhgd1v2qjih9iabiie1jtfg.summary: Locate alternative sources for the meetings with Thomas and Larry—the provided work notes contain no references to either individual—leaving a critical gap in your preparation. Plan to address these missing updates—specifically regarding Thomas's strategic input and Larry's alignment on technical integrations—by sourcing status updates directly from your communication channels. Resolve these documentation gaps prior to the 1:1—ensuring you present a complete operational picture to Michael and Mark.
tasks: Search external communication history—including email and chat logs—to reconstruct the key outcomes from your last meeting with Thomas.
Contact Larry directly—or review shared team repositories—to extract the action items and decisions from your most recent alignment session.
Flag the missing documentation—specifically the Thomas and Larry updates—as priority items to resolve before the series end on June 5, 2026.
Draft a brief progress update—highlighting dependencies and unresolved items from both workstreams—to guide the discussion with Michael and Mark.
av2mhgd1v2qjih9iabiie1jtfg.title: Monthly 1:1 w/Michael Hallock | Mark Francis (series end 6/5/26)

tkbg51ibq5i5vtklkv8pa69pao.summary: No work notes matched your recent meetings with Jon or Praveena—the search of your Drive folder returned no documents. You must pivot your preparation to the active operational bottlenecks—specifically the AWS MSR desktop pilot friction and the ongoing Integration Architect skill-gap concerns. Address these unresolved program risks directly with Claire to establish clear alignment.
tasks: Request clarification on Jon's and Praveena's current priorities—no historical notes exist in your files to guide this review.
Address the outstanding resource backfills for Matt Unsworth and Deepak—the current lack of headcount hinders tactical execution.
Align on the Integration Architect competency model—the transition of technical decision-making requires immediate skills mapping.

tkbg51ibq5i5vtklkv8pa69pao.title: Monthly 1:1 w/Claire Veitenheimer | Mark Francis

k7ssgc6tnu3s6nbbtlroh448gs.summary: Reviewing your recent work notes reveals systemic operational shifts—notably the 15–20% capacity reduction from the cybersecurity uplift and the transfer of decision-making to Integration Architects. You must navigate technical friction across key platforms—specifically the AWS desktop integration POC and the parallel Skan.ai task mining pilot. Major delivery bottlenecks persist—including unresolved backfills for critical roles and security compliance blocks on the Claims visual voicemail initiative.
tasks: Consolidate the P&C business cases across Claims, DNS, and Agency to support the Skan.ai pilot by September.
Establish a clear RACI matrix to address skill and capacity gaps for Integration Architects taking on technical decision-making.
Resolve the security and compliance hurdles blocking the integration of visual voicemail and two-way texting with HiMarley.
Enforce the new 60–90 day lead-time process for contract reviews to control vendor negotiation outcomes.
Restrict GitHub Copilot access to defined technical roles to manage enterprise credit consumption.
k7ssgc6tnu3s6nbbtlroh448gs.title: Enterprise Architecture Leadership Sync (series ends 1/...)
